title: Why I Stopped Using Social Media
summary: Opinion text — present perfect, modals, connectors.
text:
Six months ago, I decided to delete all my social media accounts. My friends thought I was crazy. "How will you know what's happening?" they asked. The truth is, I have never been happier.

I used to spend two or three hours every day scrolling on my phone. I was always tired, always comparing myself to others. I felt I had no time for the things I really wanted to do, like reading books or learning the guitar.

At first, it was strange. I kept reaching for my phone, but there was nothing to check. After two weeks, I started to feel calmer. I have read fifteen books this year. I have made new friends, real ones who I see in person.

I'm not saying everyone should do this. Social media has good things too: connecting with family abroad, finding information, supporting causes. But for me, the costs were higher than the benefits.
collocations: the costs were higher than the benefits

options: It has good and bad things
